body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Re-evaluation (2026-05-23, post-#445)

This supersedes the 2026-05-18 / earlier 2026-05-23 verdict of yes/no. The gap is now CLOSED: irrlicht tracks Bash background processes and holds the session working until they exit. Two landed changes, both present in this worktree (branch feat/record-background-process-fixture):

1. **#450 (commit b8d8e2dc) â€” background-process tracking.** The parser snapshots each background process from the structured `toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId` (+ its `tasks/<id>.output` path) and surfaces it as `SessionMetrics.BackgroundProcessCount`. The daemon gates `IsAgentDone` on `HasLiveBackgroundProcess`, holding the session `working` past `end_turn` until the process exits; an `lsof` probe on the output file is the authoritative liveness check, and a `BashOutput`-terminated status or `KillShell` drops the process.

2. **265ebf22 â€” transcript-level completion via `<task-notification>`.** The #450 completion path was written against a `queued_command` attachment shape; a claude launched under the Agent SDK harness (exactly what irrlicht's recording agent uses) suppresses BashOutput/KillShell and instead reports completion via TaskOutput + a terminal `<task-notification>` whose `<task-id>` is the `backgroundTaskId`. 265ebf22 teaches the parser to drop the tracked process on that terminal notification (both on-disk shapes: the `origin.kind` user line AND the queued_command attachment), keyed on the same `openBackgroundProcs` map. This makes the scenario recordable from inside the SDK harness â€” the daemon observes the transcript-level completion and the session settles `working â†’ ready` with no env-unset needed.

## How it was validated

Recorded against a live `claude` v2.1.150 driven through the committed recipe, observed by an `irrlichd` built from THIS worktree (version `0.4.7+265ebf2`, includes 265ebf22). The captured transcript carries: a `Bash` tool_use with `run_in_background:true` (`sleep 8 && echo BG_DONE`), a `toolUseResult.backgroundTaskId`, BashOutput polls reporting `<status>completed</status>`, and the terminal `<task-notification>`. The events.jsonl shows the exact spec arc for the primary session: `ready` at birth (+21 ms), `ready â†’ working` on the prompt, an extended `working` dwell of ~25.3 s with NO intermediate transition to ready/waiting while the bg process was alive, then a single `working â†’ ready` once it completed. `expected-validate` passes 5/5 phases.

## Why this is a clean yes (not partial)

The transcript-level completion path means the daemon no longer depends solely on the lsof liveness fallback to release the session â€” the SDK-harness `<task-notification>` is an actual transcript signal that decrements the count and re-classifies. All three user-observable spec signals are now real: `BackgroundProcessCount > 0` while alive, session held `working` past end_turn, and a single `working â†’ ready` once the last bg process exits.
caveats: agent_supports is yes â€” confirmed against the live claude v2.1.150 binary: run_in_background Bash parameter + BashOutput + KillShell + backgroundTaskId all present in the strings scan.

irrlicht_observes flipped no â†’ yes: #450 (b8d8e2dc) added BackgroundProcessCount + the working-hold gated on HasLiveBackgroundProcess, and 265ebf22 added recognition of the terminal <task-notification> completion shape the SDK-harnessed claude actually emits. Validated 5/5 by a fresh recording from a daemon built from this worktree (0.4.7+265ebf2).

The recording was captured in isolated mode (run-cell.sh without --attach) so the observing daemon was built from THIS worktree (265ebf22), not the user's separately-running daemon. Daemon version baked into manifest.json is 0.4.7+265ebf2.

Re-record variance: exact working dwell length and BashOutput poll count vary with model pacing; the spec's bg_alive_working assertion is a â‰Ą4s lower bound well inside the 8s sleep, so timing jitter never trips it.

sources.note: Adds background-process tracking: parser snapshots backgroundTaskId into BackgroundProcessCount; daemon gates IsAgentDone on HasLiveBackgroundProcess (lsof liveness probe on tasks/<id>.output) to hold the session working past end_turn; BashOutput-terminated / KillShell drop the process.

Recognizes the SDK-harness completion shape: a terminal <task-notification> (both the origin.kind user-line shape and the queued_command attachment shape) whose <task-id> == backgroundTaskId drops the tracked process from the open set, so the session settles workingâ†’ready via a transcript signal â€” no lsof-only dependency, replay-compatible. This is what makes the scenario recordable from inside the recording agent's own SDK harness.
Fresh recording: daemon_version 0.4.7+265ebf2 (built from this worktree, includes 265ebf22), expected_pass_rate 5/5 phases passed.

5/5 phases pass: session_birth (ready +21ms), pid_bind (+21ms), turn_start (readyâ†’working), bg_alive_working (~25.3s working dwell, no transition to ready/waiting while alive), turn_end (workingâ†’ready at +25265ms, under the 60s max and after the 8s sleep).
Confirms agent_supports: yes â€” claude v2.1.150 ships run_in_background (Bash param), backgroundTaskId (34 hits), BashOutput (14 hits), KillShell (8 hits).
